test_description: Validates that EA rejects new trades when equity drawdown exceeds MAX_DRAWDOWN_PERCENT limit

signals.event_title: Reference Signal: Set up for drawdown simulation
signals.setup_note: This position will experience adverse price movement to simulate drawdown

signals.event_title: Signal After Drawdown Exceeded - Should Be Rejected
signals.rationale: Attempt new trade when equity drawdown limit exceeded

test_procedure: Wait for or manually trigger a price movement that creates drawdown
Target: equity drops to $8,500 or below (15% drawdown from balance)
Check EA log for drawdown rejection message
success_criteria: After equity drawdown exceeds 15%, signal 2 is rejected
EA log contains drawdown limit rejection message
Message includes actual drawdown percentage and limit
No new trades initiated once drawdown limit exceeded

difference_from_daily_loss.daily_loss: Uses (equity - balance) calculation = floating P&L only
difference_from_daily_loss.drawdown: Uses (balance - equity) calculation = includes unrealized losses in open positions
difference_from_daily_loss.key_difference: Daily loss measures closed profit/loss; drawdown measures total account deterioration

edge_cases: Test exactly at limit (15%) - should accept
Test just below limit (14.9%) - should accept
Test just above limit (15.1%) - should reject
Test with multiple open positions in drawdown
Test recovery: once drawdown falls below limit, trading should resume
manual_setup_steps: 2. Set MAX_DRAWDOWN_PERCENT to 15 in EA parameters
5. Wait for or create adverse price movement
6. Monitor equity in MT5 Account Summary
7. Once equity drops to $8,500 or below (15% drawdown), send second signal

price_movement_guidance.eurusd: If BUY at 1.10, need price to fall to ~1.0885 (150 pips) for significant loss
price_movement_guidance.alternative: Use pending orders or market orders on opposing signals to force drawdown
